statements: A decrease in interest rates causes an increase in investment spending.
An increase in government spending leads to a rise in aggregate demand.
A rise in inflation expectations causes an increase in nominal wages.
A decrease in government spending leads to a reduction in aggregate demand.
An increase in taxes causes a decrease in disposable income.
Expansionary fiscal policy leads to an increase in interest rates.
A decrease in the money supply leads to an increase in interest rates.
An increase in interest rates causes a decrease in aggregate demand.
A decrease in aggregate demand leads to a reduction in inflation.
A decrease in aggregate demand causes a recession.
An increase in interest rates leads to a decrease in investment.
A decline in consumer confidence causes a reduction in consumption.
- An increase in aggregate demand causes an increase in the general price level.
- A decrease in the money supply leads to a decrease in inflation.
- An increase in production costs causes an increase in prices.
A decrease in aggregate demand causes an increase in unemployment.
An increase in labor supply leads to an increase in unemployment.
A decrease in labor productivity causes an increase in unemployment.
Investment in human capital causes economic growth.
Increased productivity leads to higher economic growth.
Technological innovation causes increased economic output.
A country's comparative advantage in producing a good causes it to specialize in that good.
A decrease in tariffs leads to an increase in international trade.
A depreciation of a country's currency causes its exports to become more competitive.
A decrease in interest rates causes an increase in stock prices.
An increase in inflation expectations leads to a rise in bond yields.
A surge in market volatility causes a decrease in investor confidence.
- High levels of public debt can lead to increased interest rates.
- High public debt can cause a reduction in private investment.
- High public debt can lead to a decrease in economic growth.
A higher interest rate causes a currency to appreciate.
An increase in inflation leads to a depreciation of the currency.
A rise in the terms of trade causes a currency to appreciate.
